$schema_note: Edit this file to add/remove projects from the portfolio hub. Fields: slug (URL slug, also folder name under public/portfolio/<slug>/); repo (owner/name on GitHub); featured (bool — pins to hero row); order (number — featured row ordering, lower = leftmost); url (optional live deployment URL); tagline (optional override of the GitHub description). Everything else — description, language, stars, topics, commits — is pulled live by scripts/sync-portfolio.mjs.

projects.tagline: Live 3.8B-param forward pass in the browser with mech-interp ablation.
projects.impact: Runs a 3.8-billion-parameter AI model live in your browser and lets you watch — and tweak — how it thinks, layer by layer.

projects.tagline: WebGPU quantum many-body + chemistry simulator. Brute-force verified element-wise against PySCF, with WebGPU acceleration on the CCSD(T) bottleneck.
projects.impact: Does research-grade quantum chemistry inside a browser tab, and checks its own answers against the field's reference tool, number for number.

projects.tagline: The editor you're using right now.
projects.impact: The markdown editor this entire portfolio is written and rendered in.

projects.tagline: Federated SGD over Cloudflare Durable Objects. 50% Byzantine tolerance.
projects.impact: Trains a shared AI model across many machines that never swap raw data — and keeps converging even when half of them lie.

projects.tagline: TR-MRV-Bench — open emissions benchmark beating EU CBAM by 85.9%.
projects.impact: An open benchmark for industrial carbon emissions that's dramatically more accurate than the EU's default border-tax estimate.

projects.tagline: Local-first grammar + writing assistant for macOS. Native system-wide overlay across every app. Harper rules + on-device Gemma/BitNet via QVAC Fabric. 100% on-device, zero cloud.
projects.impact: Fixes your grammar in every app on a Mac without sending a single word to the cloud.

projects.tagline: Drop-in npm install replacement that sandboxes postinstall scripts, blocks network access, detects typosquats, and catches supply-chain attacks before they execute.
projects.impact: Installs npm packages safely by sandboxing the hidden install scripts attackers use to slip in malware.

projects.tagline: Multi-agent rights advisor for APJ migrant workers — five specialists read your contract in your language, visibly disagree, then converge. Databricks APJ hackathon entry.
projects.impact: Five AI specialists read a migrant worker's contract in their own language, openly disagree, then reach a verdict on their rights.

projects.tagline: Real WebGPU compute benchmarks running on visitors' hardware — kernel-fusion validated across hundreds of devices and seven GPU vendors.
projects.impact: Measures real GPU speed on whatever device you visit from, pooled across hundreds of machines and seven GPU brands.

projects.tagline: Phi-3-mini running in a browser on 10 hand-written WGSL kernels. No TVM, no compiler, no API key — within striking distance of the autotuned baseline for ~40× less code.
projects.impact: Runs a full language model in your browser from ten hand-written GPU programs — no giant compiler stack, no API key.

projects.tagline: Open scientific questions in plain words — a 3D lab where a researcher answers anything, draws it on a whiteboard, ties it to a real benchmark. Browser-only, bring your own LLM.
projects.impact: Ask any open scientific question in plain words; a 3D researcher answers it, sketches it on a whiteboard, and ties it to real data.

projects.tagline: Radiobiology Monte Carlo in a browser tab — WebGPU port of CNRS Geant4-DNA. Karamitros IRT chemistry + 21×21 B-DNA fiber-grid scoring, cross-checked against the canonical reference.
projects.impact: Simulates how radiation damages DNA — a browser port of a CERN-grade physics tool, cross-checked against the original.
Four playable demos where AI agents evolve in your browser and trade their 'genes' directly with other players, no server in the middle.
Shows that collapsing an AI model's entire inner loop into one GPU call removes the overhead that dominates small-model speed in the browser.
Fuses a self-driving-car sensor pipeline into a single GPU pass — many times faster than the standard PyTorch version on the same chip.
Proves the single-GPU-call trick still works on a 3.6-billion-parameter model, with a large measured speedup.
Runs the exact same GPU code with and without a browser to measure precisely what the browser costs you.
The first paper of the series, showing one fused GPU call beats many small ones for evolutionary search.

projects.tagline: Chiang Mai burning-season simulation — the project that triggered the kernel-fusion line.
projects.impact: A simulation of Chiang Mai's smoke-season air pollution — the project that kick-started the whole GPU-speed research line.

projects.tagline: Browser-native LoRA fine-tuning of BitNet b1.58 2B4T — first public demo of training a ternary 2B LLM in a web page.
projects.impact: The first public demo of fine-tuning a 2-billion-parameter AI model entirely inside a web page.
A browser engine that trains image-recognition models from scratch on your own GPU — the vision counterpart to fused-lora.
A tiny local server that streams my knowledge graph and running agents into the browser in real time.
Turns a folder of notes into a navigable 3D galaxy where related ideas cluster by meaning.

projects.tagline: Privacy layer between any app and any third-party LLM — pseudonymizes emails / paths / URLs / IPs deterministically before the wire and reverses the mapping on the response.
projects.impact: Strips names, emails and IPs out of your text before it reaches any AI service, then restores them in the reply.
Gives an AI assistant a body you can visit and talk to inside a 3D space.
Lets AI tools like Claude Desktop and Cursor safely read your local files — read-only, and you choose what's shared.

projects.tagline: Local-first browser brain — SolidJS + WebGPU on Cloudflare. Persistent agent in the right rail; privacy layer chooses which model sees what before the message ships.
projects.impact: A persistent AI sidekick living in your browser that decides which model is allowed to see what before any message is sent.

projects.tagline: Realtime LIF simulation of the FlyWire connectome + MANC spine + flybody body — a fly brain talking to a fly body, live in your browser via WebGPU + WASM.
projects.impact: Runs a real fruit-fly brain wired to a fruit-fly body, live in your browser — a tiny digital animal.
The browser-AI speed research packaged as a drop-in npm library other developers can install.

projects.tagline: Online context-healing loop for long-running LLM agents — tag-then-rebuild compression preserves cross-chunk coreference instead of the cliff-edge auto-compact.
projects.impact: Keeps long-running AI agents coherent by compressing their memory without losing the thread, instead of abruptly forgetting.
Generates images from a text prompt in real time as you type, running the diffusion model on your own GPU.

projects.tagline: Research umbrella for GPU kernel fusion — eliminating per-dispatch overhead across evolutionary computation and transformer inference. Two preprints.
projects.impact: The umbrella research program — two papers — behind making GPUs faster by fusing many small steps into one.
A living medieval village where 15 AI villagers follow daily routines and stumble into emergent dramas.
A Turkish metal-shop app that turns a photo of a drawing into a priced quote in about a minute, fully offline on the phone.
Copy-paste replacements for popular npm packages so you own the code and ship zero dependencies.

projects.tagline: Privacy-preserving distributed AI via browser-based Evolutionary Strategies — measured Byzantine tolerance at 50% attacker share.
projects.impact: Trains AI across many browsers that never share private data, and stays correct even when half are actively cheating.

projects.tagline: Directed-evolution engine for dual-function enzymes — PETase + metallothionein fusion for a Chiang Mai water filter.
projects.impact: Evolves a two-in-one enzyme design aimed at a low-cost water filter for Chiang Mai.

projects.tagline: Browser-based protein sequence evolution via WebGPU. Honest about proxy-fitness scope (no wet-lab validation).
projects.impact: Evolves protein sequences in the browser — and is honest that it's a computational proxy, not wet-lab proven.

projects.tagline: Aggregation strategies for distributed evolution — six political-system metaphors tested on crypto trading.
projects.impact: Tests six ways of combining distributed AI 'votes', each modeled on a political system, on a crypto-trading task.

projects.tagline: Multi-agent advisory panel for early-stage founders — four AI agents discuss your idea in a 3D meeting room. Local LLM, no cloud, no telemetry.
projects.impact: Four AI advisors debate your startup idea in a 3D meeting room, on a local model with no cloud and no telemetry.

projects.tagline: Local-first AI vault for Turkish attorneys — KVKK-compliant: müvekkil dosyaları cihazdan çıkmaz. Derivative of markview targeting Turkish solo/small-firm lawyers.
projects.impact: A private case-file vault for Turkish lawyers where client files never leave the device, meeting strict privacy law.
